Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails for stipend/expense complaints in Julian's inbox using search_emails (queries like "stipend", "standing desk", "expense"). Discover Maria Santos' complaint about her $550 standing desk auto-rejection and mention of Lisa Park's $130 WiFi issue. Discover Julian's reply promising a fix by end of week plus a $50 courtesy credit. Discover Tamara Byrd's email requesting written confirmation about Andre Washington's $400 desk overage and Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking passes. Discover Rachel Whitfield's follow-up to Mina (CC Julian) connecting three unresolved Vectral stipend issues to the Q3 renewal negotiation.
- Search Slack #customer-support channel (C007) using conversations_history or conversations_search_messages for stipend-related messages. Discover Julian's April 8 message flagging the Maria Santos complaint, his $50 courtesy credit promise, and three unresolved Vectral rejections. Discover Zara's April 14 message about James Okafor calling about his $82 keyboard tray rejection. Discover Omar's April 15 message about Kevin Tran's $250 coworking rejection, noting he believes it was correctly rejected because Vectral provides desk space at Denver HQ. Discover Mina's March 31 message noting she manually approved two incorrectly rejected Vectral expense claims (she does not name the specific employees in this message). Discover from Julian's April 8 message that the two corrected employees were Danielle Osei (chair) and Kevin Tran (internet).
- Look up Vectral stipend records in Airtable Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records. Discover three records flagged "Pending manual correction": Maria Santos standing desk $550 (recStip000000018), James Okafor keyboard tray $82 (recStip000000019), Lisa Park WiFi $130 (recStip000000020). Also discover Kevin Tran coworking $250 (recStip000000005) which was correctly rejected per policy -- review notes say "Vectral provides dedicated desk space at Denver HQ" with no manual correction flag. Also discover Sarah Kim standing desk $480 (recStip000000024) which initially looks like another false rejection (same stale $400 default limit) but review notes say "Claim submitted after employee separation date (March 8, 2026). Rejection upheld by Mina Hashimoto on March 18" -- this rejection should NOT be corrected. Discover two records already corrected by Mina: Danielle Osei $475 chair and Kevin Tran $185 internet.
- Look up Mosaic stipend records in Airtable using same tool. Discover Andre Washington standing desk $1,200 approved in error with $400 overage being absorbed per Elena's directive (recStip000000021), Andre Washington coworking $350 approved in error and absorbed (recStip000000022), Kenji Nakamura coworking $135 approved in error and absorbed (recStip000000023). All three already handled -- no corrections needed, but Tamara has not received written confirmation.
- Look up Vectral Systems renewal deal in CRM using crm_search_deals. Discover deal "Vectral Systems, Q3 2026 Contract Renewal (At Risk)" at dealstage "at_risk" (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk). Rachel Whitfield demanded 15% discount citing ExpenseBot failures. Confirms Rachel is using stipend issues as renewal leverage.
- Check Linear for existing ExpenseBot tickets using linear_list_issues. Find multiple engineering tickets in progress ([P1] Fix Vectral config, [P1] Fix Mosaic config, full audit of 47 expenses, pilot monitoring) but no ticket tracking the customer-facing support fallout.
- Update Maria Santos stipend record (recStip000000018) in Airtable using airtable_update_records. Set "Approval Status" to "Approved" and "Approved Amount" to 550.
- Update James Okafor stipend record (recStip000000019) using airtable_update_records. Set "Approval Status" to "Approved" and "Approved Amount" to 82.
- Update Lisa Park stipend record (recStip000000020) using airtable_update_records. Set "Approval Status" to "Approved" and "Approved Amount" to 130.
- Create a Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or similar) to track the ExpenseBot customer support fallout. Title references ExpenseBot support fallout or stipend complaint tracking (or similar).
- Send email using send_email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with the full Vectral stipend rundown. Content names Maria Santos, James Okafor, and Lisa Park as affected employees, describes what corrections were made, and flags Rachel Whitfield's April 21 escalation connecting the stipend failures to the Q3 renewal.
- Send email using send_email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com confirming how MoveOps is handling the Mosaic expense issues. Content confirms Andre Washington's $400 standing desk overage is being absorbed by MoveOps, and Kenji Nakamura will not face a clawback for the $135 coworking passes.
- Post summary in #customer-support (C007) using conversations_add_message. Message names which Vectral employees had records corrected (Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park), notes what is pending, and gives Zara and Omar guidance on handling further stipend calls.
- Log a CRM engagement on the Vectral Systems account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_vectral"]). Note documents the ExpenseBot stipend support follow-up and its connection to the Q3 2026 renewal risk.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record) to retrieve stipend transaction records from table tblStipends00001 in base appMoveOpsOps001.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_search_records twice on base appMoveOpsOps001, table 'Stipend Transactions', once for Vectral and once for Mosaic. The tool returned records from tblStipends00001 as confirmed by the table_id field in the results.
✓Run #2The agent called `airtable_search_records` with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' for both Vectral and Mosaic accounts. This directly queries the tblStipends00001 table in base appMoveOpsOps001.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_search_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' (which is tblStipends00001) twice — once for Vectral and once for Mosaic records. The tool returned actual stipend records including recStip000000018, 19, 20, etc.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' for both Vectral and Mosaic accounts, retrieving records from tblStipends00001 as confirmed by the results showing tblStipends00001 as the table_id.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_search_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' (mapping to tblStipends00001) twice — once filtering for Vectral and once for Mosaic. Both calls returned results and informed the investigation.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_search_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' (which maps to tblStipends00001 as confirmed by the update call's table_id). This was done twice - once for Vectral and once for Mosaic accounts.
2 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) to check for existing ExpenseBot-related tickets.
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent called linear_list_teams but never called linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to check for existing ExpenseBot-related tickets before creating a new one. It went directly to linear_create_issue without checking for existing tickets.
✗Run #2The agent called `linear_list_teams` but never called `linear_list_issues` or `linear_get_issue` to check for existing ExpenseBot-related tickets before creating a new one. The criterion requires checking for existing tickets first.
✗Run #3The agent called linear_list_teams but never called linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to check for existing ExpenseBot-related tickets before creating a new one. It jumped directly to linear_create_issue.
✗Run #4The agent called linear_list_teams but never called linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to check for existing ExpenseBot-related tickets before creating a new one. The agent went straight to linear_create_issue without checking for duplicates.
✗Run #5The agent called linear_list_teams but never called linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to check whether existing ExpenseBot-related tickets already existed before creating a new one. It proceeded directly to linear_create_issue without checking for duplicates.
✗Run #6The agent called linear_list_teams but never called linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to check for existing ExpenseBot-related tickets before creating a new one. It went straight to linear_create_issue without checking for existing tickets.
3 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_deals, crm_search_companies, or crm_get_deal) to look up the Vectral Systems account or renewal deal status.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'Vectral', which returned the Vectral Systems account (company_vectral). It also called crm_list_engagements for company_vectral and crm_search_contacts for Rachel Whitfield.
✓Run #2The agent called `crm_search_companies` with name 'Vectral' and also called `crm_get_company` with id 'company_vectral' and `crm_list_engagements` for the Vectral account. Multiple CRM lookups were performed on the Vectral account.
✓Run #3The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'Vectral', then crm_get_company with id 'company_vectral', and crm_search_deals with company_id 'company_vectral'. All returned successfully with Vectral account data.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'Vectral' early in the trajectory and also called crm_list_engagements with company_id 'company_vectral', successfully retrieving the Vectral Systems account.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'Vectral' early in the trajectory and also called crm_list_engagements and crm_get_contact on the Vectral account, establishing the renewal context.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_companies for 'Vectral' and crm_search_deals with company_id 'company_vectral', both of which look up the Vectral Systems account and deal status in the CRM.
4 The Airtable stipend record for Maria Santos (recStip000000018 in table tblStipends00001) was updated via airtable_update_records to change the Approval Status field from 'Rejected' to 'Approved', and the tool returned a success response.
